paper_metadata.title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
paper_metadata.abstract: In this work, we develop and release Llama 2, a collection of pretrained and fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) ranging in scale from 7 billion to 70 billion parameters. Our fine-tuned LLMs, called Llama 2-Chat, are optimized for dialogue use cases. Our models outperform open-source chat models on most benchmarks we tested, and based on our human evaluations for helpfulness and safety, may be a suitable substitute for closed-source models. We provide a detailed description of our approach to fine-tuning and safety improvements of Llama 2-Chat in order to enable the community to build on our work and contribute to the responsible development of LLMs.

extraction_data.innovation: Llama 3.1 is an open-source, fine-tuned language model family with 7B, 175B, and 70B parameters, designed for chat and general language tasks. It uses a Transformer architecture and is trained on a large text corpus. The innovation lies in its open release, enabling transparency and democratizing access to large language models.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) with high-quality instruction-tuning data and RLHF
extraction_data.optimizer: Cosine learning rate schedule with initial learning rate 2×10⁻⁵, weight decay 0.1, batch size 64, sequence length 4096
extraction_data.extension: Rejection Sampling and PPO fine-tuning with iterative updates to improve multi-turn consistency and safety
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language models for chat applications with human preference alignment
extraction_data.application: Chat, instruction following, general language tasks
